title: Emergent Capabilities in Frontier Models
concept: Frontier models scale parameters and tokens beyond the Chinchilla limit, unlocking emergent coding, math, and logical reasoning.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary factor that causes new capabilities to emerge in frontier AI models?

options.text: Human code rules are manually written for every skill

exercises.explain: Frontier models scale parameters and tokens beyond previous limits, unlocking new capabilities like logic and coding.

cards.title: Hidden Superpowers
cards.body: Have you ever seen an AI suddenly solve a complex logic puzzle or write computer code, when a smaller version of the same AI could barely write a sentence? Let's discover how scaling up models unlocks these surprise 'superpowers'!

cards.title: What are Emergent Capabilities?
cards.body: Emergent capabilities are advanced skills (like writing code, solving math riddles, or logic reasoning) that suddenly appear in AI models only after they grow past a certain size, even though they weren't specifically programmed to do them.

cards.title: The Reading Breakthrough
cards.body: Think of a child learning to read. At first, they struggle with single words. But once they know enough words (scaling), they suddenly unlock a brand new ability: they can read whole books, understand jokes, and follow complex stories. The child didn't get new brain hardware; they just reached a scale where reading 'emerged'.

cards.title: Scaling Up to Code
cards.body: A medium-sized AI might fail completely at solving hard calculus problems or coding a full video game. However, when we increase its size 10 times, it suddenly starts getting these complex tasks correct. The ability 'emerges' at scale.

cards.title: Did We Code That?
cards.body: People think AI developers must write custom code to teach models how to reason logically. In reality, developers just give the model more data and computer power (scaling), and the reasoning skills emerge all on their own!

cards.title: The Scale Shift
cards.body: Compare how capabilities change when scaling a model from small to giant size.

left.text: Can only do simple text completion and basic word matching.

right.text: Unlocks emergent coding, multi-step math, and logical reasoning.

exercises.prompt: What is the best description of 'emergent capabilities' in AI?

options.text: Skills that appear suddenly only after a model is scaled to a very large size
Basic features like predicting the very next word in a short sentence
Manual programming rules added by human software developers
Errors and bugs that cause the AI model to crash during training

exercises.explain: Emergent capabilities are behaviors that are not present in smaller models but suddenly appear when the model scale (size, data, compute) is significantly increased.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Emergent capabilities like complex math reasoning are easily performed by even the smallest AI models.
exercises.explain: Emergent capabilities are unpredictable and only appear after a model crosses a certain scale threshold. Small models do not possess them.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the concept of how emergent capabilities are unlocked.
exercises.sentence: Emergent capabilities in frontier models are unlocked by scaling up parameters and training data.
exercises.explain: Scaling up parameters and data is the primary driver that unlocks sudden emergent behaviors in AI models.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following represents an emergent capability in a frontier model?

options.text: An AI that simply autocompletes the word 'apple' after 'red'
An AI that debugs a complex Python script and explains the logic step-by-step
An AI that repeats a user's typed input back to them word-for-word

exercises.explain: AI outputs that show multi-step logic, planning, or writing working code are generated by advanced frontier models using emergent reasoning. Standard text completion is common to small models.

exercises.prompt: How do frontier models gain emergent capabilities like advanced reasoning and programming?

options.text: Engineers write custom code rules for each new math or programming skill.
The model naturally develops them as a side effect of scaling compute and parameters.
The model is physically rebuilt using a completely different processor hardware.
The model copies the exact answers from a static database of pre-calculated problems.

exercises.explain: Emergent capabilities are not programmed manually. They appear automatically as a side effect of scaling up parameters, data size, and training compute past critical thresholds.
